original: Russia lands Lunokhod 1 unmanned remote-controlled vehicle on Moon
Douglas Engelbartreceives the patent for the first computer mouse
Leonids meteor shower peaks (150,000+ per hour)
Earth's hottest day: global average surface temperature more than 2°C (2.06°C) above pre-industrial levels for the first time according to EU's Copernicus Climate Change Service
Smaller pyramid find within 2 known Kukulkan "nesting" pyramids announced at Chichen Itza in Yucatan, Mexico, dating to 550-80 AD
Time White and T. Assebework discover the first fossils of 2.5 million year old Australopithecus garhi, an ancestor on the human family tree, in Bouri, Middle Awash, Ethiopia
David Livingstonebecomes the first European to see Victoria Falls in what is now Zambia and Zimbabwe
The first ship sails through the Panama Canal, connecting the Atlantic and Pacific oceans
Suez Canal in Egypt opens, linking the Mediterranean and Red seas
New world air speed record 606 mph (975 kph) set by HJ Wilson of RAF
Surveyor 6 becomes 1st man-made object to lift off Moon
Abraham Lincolnbegins first draft of his Gettysburg Address
Elizabeth I, aged 25, ascends the English throne upon the death of her half-sister, Queen Mary I
Nicolas Appert, French inventor of airtight food preservation (food canning, bouillon tablet), born in Châlons-en-Champagne, France (d. 1841)
August Ferdinand Möbius, German mathematician and astronomer, discovered the Möbius strip, born in Schulpforta, Saxony (d. 1868)
Garbiñe Muguruza becomes first Spanish player to win WTA Finals defeating Estonian Anett Kontaveit 6–3, 7–5 in Zapopan, Mexico
Kirk Fogg, American TV host of "Legends of the Hidden Temple", born in Los Angeles, California
Coyote [Nathaniel] Peterson, American YouTube personality (Brave Wilderness), born in Newbury, Ohio
Official naming of element 111, Roentgenium (Rg).
Beginning of a 5 day New England Blizzard that covers thousands of houses and kills hundreds
Missing Argentine naval submarine ARA San Juan with 44 on board found on sea floor a year after it disappeared off coast of Argentina
Rival cities of Buda & Pest unite to form the capital of Hungary
Erling Kagge begins successful exploration at South pole
Syracuse fullbackJim Brown, scores NCAA record of 43 pts (vs Colgate)
Jeff Buckley, American musician and singer ("Hallelujah"), born in Anaheim, California (d. 1997)
[Clarke] Isaac Hanson, American pop singer and guitarist (Hanson - "MMMbop"), born in Tulsa, Oklahoma
Shanica Knowles, American actress known for "Hannah Montana", born in Miami, Florida
Kate Douglass, American swimmer (Olympic gold 200m breaststroke, 4×100m medley 2024), born in Pelham, New York
Ireland beats Rugby World Cup champions New Zealand, 16-9 at Aviva Stadium, Dublin; first victory over the All Blacks ever on home soil; flyhalf Johnny Sexton kicks 3 penalties and a conversion
German Red Bull driverSebastian Vettelwins a record 8th consecutive Formula 1 race with victory in the US Grand Prix at the Circuit of the Americas
21-year-old Greek tennis star Stefanos Tsitsipas beatsDominic Thiemof Austria 6-7 (6-8), 6-2, 7-6 (7-4) in London to become the youngest winner of the ATP Finals in 18 years
Australian Leyton Hewitt scores back-to-back season-ending Tennis Masters Cup titles with a classic 7–5, 7–5, 2–6, 2–6, 6–4 victory over SpaniardJuan Carlos Ferreroin Shanghai, China
Ryk Neethling, South African swimmer (Olympic gold 4x100m freestyle 2004; World C'ship SC gold x 3; WR 100m IM SC 51.52), born in Bloemfontein, South Africa
Yusuf Pathan, Indian cricket all-rounder (57 ODIs, 2 x 100, HS 123no, 33 wickets; winner ICC T20 World Cup 2007, ICC World Cup 2011), born in Baroda, India
Aaron Finch, Australian cricket batsman (5 Tests, 2 x 50; captain Men's T20 World Cup 2021; record score T20I 172 2018; Victoria) and broadcaster (Fox Sports, Network 7), born in Colac, Australia
Elías Díaz, Venezuelan baseball catcher (MVP MLB All-Star Game 2023 Colorado Rockies; Pittsburgh Pirates), born in Maracaibo, Venezuela
Roland Matthes, German swimmer (Olympic gold 100, 200m backstroke 1968, 72; WR x 19), born in Pößneck, Thuringia, Germany
Philadelphia Flyers win 13th straight NHL game
In an all-American finalPete Sampraswins his first of 5 ATP Tour World Championship tennis titles with a 3–6, 7–6, 6–3, 6–4 victory overJim Courierin Frankfurt, Germany
Mario Lemieuxenters NHL Hall of Fame
SF Giants future Baseball Hall of Fame sluggerWillie McCoveywins NL Rookie of the Year
NBA NY Knick Bill Cartwright, ties record of 19 of 19 free throws
Bonnie Bryant becomes the only left-handed player in history to win an LPGA Tour event; scores a 3-stroke victory in the Bill Branch Golf Classic in Fort Myers, Florida
Yolanda King, American actress (Fluke, Ghosts of Mississippi) and activist, daughter of Martin Luther King Jr., born in Montgomery, Alabama (d. 2007)
Ronnie Devoe, American pop and R&B vocalist (New Edition - "Cool It Now"; Bell Biv Devoe - "Do Me!"), born in Boston, Massachusetts
Reggie Wayne, American football wide receiver (Super Bowl XLI; First-team All-Pro 2010 Indianapolis Colts; 6 x Pro Bowl), born in New Orleans, Louisiana
Sarolta Monspart, Hungarian orienteering competitor (first non-Scandinavian female to win World C'ship 1972; 14 x national titles), born in Budapest, Hungary (d. 2021)
Bob Mathias, American decathlete (Olympic gold 1948, 52) and congressman, born in Tulare, California (d. 2006)
Christopher Paolini, American novelist known for "The Inheritance Cycle", born in Los Angeles, California
Giannis Bourousis, Greek basketball center (EuroLeague C'ship 2015 Real Madrid; All-EuroLeague First Team 2009, 16; Greek League HOF), born in Karditsa, Greece
Kimya Dawson, American folk singer (Moldy Peaches - "Anyone Else but You"), born in Bedford Hills, New York
Lord Infamous [Ricky T Dunigan], American rapper (Three 6 Mafia), born in Memphis, Tennessee
Robin Li [Li Yanhong], Chinese founder of the Chinese search engine Baidu, born in Yangquan, Shanxi
Ryotaro Okiayu, Japanese voice actor for Lee Chaolan, and Byakuya Kuchiki, born in  Kitakyushu, Japan
Daisy Fuentes, Cuban-American VJ and TV presenter (America's Funniest Videos), born in Havana, Cuba
Jack Vettriano, Scottish painter (The Singing Butler), born in Methil, Scotland
Martin Barre, British rock guitarist (Jethro Tull - "Aqualung"), born in Kings Heath, Birmingham, England
Peter Cook, British comic and actor (Bedazzled: Yellowbeard; The Princess Bride), born in Torquay England (d. 1995)

David Amram, American classical, jazz, folk, and film score composer (Shakespeare in the Park; Splendor in the Grass; The Manchurian Candidate), musician, conductor, and educator, born in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania
